ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite beef brisket and chicken nuggets energy bowl. FOODON_00002473 (beef dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Beef, cooked) used as closest parent. No composite bowl code exists in FoodEx2 for this mixed beef brisket and chicken nuggets meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Beef dish) used as closest parent for this composite meal. No specific code for beef brisket black pepper nuggets energy bowl.

serving.natural_language_portion: One energy bowl meal typically weighs approximately 450 g and includes a grain base, braised beef brisket, black pepper chicken nuggets, and accompanying vegetables or sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Beef brisket braised and fried chicken nuggets composite bowl — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Beef brisket braised (FDC ID 174032), Chicken nuggets (FDC ID 175148), cooked white rice (FDC ID 168878), and black pepper sauce analogue
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA entry exists for this specific composite restaurant bowl. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging: approximately 35% braised beef brisket, 25% fried chicken nuggets, 30% cooked white rice, 10% vegetables and black pepper sauce. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used for each component. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white rice and mixed meat-rice composite analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific restaurant bowl. GI estimated at 56 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being white rice (GI ~72 per University of Sydney GI Database) blended with a substantial protein and fat load from braised beef brisket and fried chicken nuggets, which attenuates the overall glycemic response. Mixed rice-meat bowl analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database range from GI 45–65. A midpoint estimate of 56 (medium GI) is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 83.25) / 100 ≈ 46.6, rounded to 46.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1890 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this bowl due to its high sodium content from black pepper sauce and processed nuggets, which can significantly contribute to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 56) driven by white rice base; moderate carbohydrate load per serving (~83 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with portion size as the white rice base contributes a medium GI and a high glycemic load per serving (~46); pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables and reducing the rice portion can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket and fried nuggets (~2.9 g per 100 g) and high sodium from sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit this meal to occasional consumption due to its combined saturated fat and sodium load; choosing a smaller portion and requesting reduced sauce can help lower cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Beef Brisket Black Pepper Nuggets Energy Bowl is a Western-Asian fusion bowl meal served at casual dining and fast-casual restaurants in Singapore, including Subway and Mix & Match outlets. The bowl typically consists of a base of steamed white rice or grains topped with slow-braised beef brisket seasoned in a savoury black pepper sauce and accompanied by deep-fried chicken nuggets. The combination delivers a hearty, protein-rich meal with contrasting textures: the tender, fall-apart braised brisket against the crispy exterior of the nuggets. The black pepper sauce provides a pungent, mildly spicy, and umami-rich flavour profile characteristic of Singaporean-style Western fusion cooking. Nutritionally, the bowl is energy-dense at approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution (~12.8 g per 100 g) from both the beef and chicken components. The sodium content is elevated (~420 mg per 100 g) due to the black pepper sauce and processed nugget seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index (~56) is driven by the white rice base but is partially attenuated by the protein and fat content of the meat components. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and contains common allergens including gluten, soy, and egg from the nugget coating. It is a filling, satisfying lunch or dinner option popular among working adults in Singapore seeking a convenient, flavourful, and protein-forward meal.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA entry exists for this specific composite restaurant bowl. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for braised beef brisket, fried chicken nuggets, cooked white rice, and black pepper sauce analogue. GI is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI attenuated by protein and fat load. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are individually well-supported by USDA data.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source component data. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source component data. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source components rather than confirmed zero values. Nutrient values represent a per-100g composite estimate for a bowl with approximately 35% braised beef brisket, 25% fried chicken nuggets, 30% cooked white rice, and 10% vegetables and black pepper sauce. Actual restaurant preparation may vary. Halal status is listed as false because Subway Singapore serves non-halal items and the specific outlet (Subway Funan) is not certified halal; consumers should verify with the restaurant. The food_id GD000376 is sourced from field survey data seed data and is not present in the FD series food index.
